item.title: Alien Earths: What Makes Us Special? - Professor Chris Lintott
item.description: Sometime in 2026, we will discover our 10000th exoplanet, a world around a distant star. This population of worlds has proved remarkably diverse,…

item.title: From Mars with Love: Postcards from 50 Years of Exploring The Red Planet - Chris Lintott
item.description: During the fifty years since the launch of the Viking spacecraft to Mars, our view of the red planet has changed from hostile desert to a world…

item.title: How It Ends: What We Know about the Fate of the Universe - Chris Lintott
item.description: How will it all end? Predicting the far future of our Universe depends on understanding its present. This lecture starts with what seems to be a…

item.title: A New Sky - Chris Lintott
item.description: The JWST is the most expensive and powerful telescope astronomers have ever constructed. Its launch in 2021 started a new phase in our exploration…

item.description: Watch the Q&A session here: https://youtu.be/TRCkgDWKTdY Mapping the stars is, perhaps, the oldest of astronomical pursuits, but it has been…

item.title: Touching the Sun - Chris Lintott
item.description: Watch the Q&A session here: https://youtu.be/pXoU-nZmhn8 Despite its familiarity, the Sun is a very different presence from the friendly yellow…

item.title: A Mirror in the Sky - Chris Lintott
item.description: The first lecture in the series considers the most famous telescope of all, the Hubble space telescope. A project more than forty years in the…

item.title: First light: Revealing the Early Universe - Chris Lintott
item.description: The final lecture in the series returns to the theme of how insight is derived from observations, considering the cosmic microwave background. This…

item.title: Is it Aliens? The Most Unusual Star In The Galaxy - Chris Lintott
item.description: Boyajian's star, a faint and unprepossessing presence in the constellation of Cygnus, attracted astronomers' attention when it began to…

item.title: Pulsars, Microwave Ovens and the Radio Sky - Chris Lintott
item.description: There have been two major revolutions in how we look at the sky - the shift beyond the optical to other wavelengths, particularly the radio, and the…
